title: Welcome
body: Welcome to the course, how it works, and what you’ll cover.

_graphic.alt: Electricians and electrical technicians at work.

title: Chapter 1 — Prepare to enter an electrotechnology workplace
displayTitle: Chapter 1 — Prepare to enter an electrotechnology workplace
body:
Understanding how to work safely in an electrotechnology environment is essential for preventing accidents and maintaining compliance with safety regulations. This chapter will introduce you to the fundamental requirements for doing electrical work.

This chapter will cover:

Obtaining work area access permits

  • You will learn about different types of work area access permits, when they are required and how to apply for them.

Following relevant safety regulations and codes

This section explains key WHS laws, regulations and codes of practice that apply to electrical work and how to comply with them.

Applying safe work methods for risk control

You will learn how to follow safe work methods to control risks and complete electrical tasks safely.

Preparing and performing isolation procedures

This section covers how to isolate electrical and non-electrical systems to prevent accidental activation during maintenance and repairs.

Checking tools, equipment and chemicals for safety

You will learn how to inspect and use tools, equipment and chemicals correctly to prevent accidents and injuries.

Using appropriate personal protective equipment

This section outlines the types of PPE used in electrical work and how to select and use them properly.

_graphic.alt: An electrician preparing to enter an electrotechnology workplace.

title: Chapter 2 — Apply safe electrotechnology working practices
displayTitle: Chapter 2 — Apply safe electrotechnology working practices
body:
Applying safe working practices in an electrotechnology environment is essential for preventing injuries and ensuring work is performed according to industry standards and safety regulations. This chapter builds on the previous section by focusing on how to identify, assess and control risks in the workplace. Understanding and following these safety practices will help you work efficiently while minimising hazards.

This chapter will cover:

Implementing risk control measures

This section explains how to apply the hierarchy of control to eliminate or reduce workplace risks.

Handling accidents, fires and emergencies

You will learn how to respond quickly and effectively to workplace emergencies, including electrical fires and accidents.

Applying safe work methods at heights

You will learn about the safety requirements for working at heights, including the use of harnesses and elevated platforms.

Using safe methods for manual handling tasks

This section explains how to lift, carry, push and pull equipment correctly to avoid strain and injury.

Demonstrating removal of an electric shock victim

You will learn the correct procedures for safely removing a person from a live electrical source.

Maintaining cleanliness in the work area

This section outlines the importance of keeping the worksite organised and free of hazards to prevent accidents.

_graphic.alt: A worksite where safe electrotechnology working practices are applied.

title: Chapter 3 — Follow electrotechnology workplace procedures
body:
Understanding how to identify hazards and apply risk controls is part of maintaining a safe and efficient work environment. In electrical work, hazards can arise from faulty equipment, poor working conditions or unsafe practices. Following workplace safety procedures helps you recognise and respond to these hazards, reducing the risk of injury and equipment damage. This chapter builds on previous discussions about workplace safety by focusing on how to apply these procedures in real-world situations.

This chapter will cover:

  • Consulting about hazards and control measures
  • You’ll learn how to actively participate in identifying hazards and reviewing control measures through workplace consultation.
  • Reporting identified hazards
  • This subchapter explains how to recognise hazards and report them to the appropriate person using workplace procedures.
  • Completing WHS/OHS documentation and incident records
  • You’ll learn how to accurately complete workplace safety records and reports in line with legal and workplace requirements.
  • Following workplace instructions
  • This subchapter covers how to follow specific workplace instructions and procedures to meet safety and legal requirements.

_graphic.alt: A team following electrotechnology workplace procedures together.
